text:
Problem Overview

In this problem, students continue to convert ratios to percent statements as a way to compare the ratios. By analyzing student work in What If...?, students are introduced to tape diagrams (also called percent bars when using them to analyze percents) and double number lines. Students also look at finding percent amounts using strategies that they have used in previous investigations, such as scaling and rate tables.

Implementation Note: You will want to have a conversation after the Initial Challenge before moving on to the What If...?. This will allow you to highlight your students' strategies before analyzing the strategies of other students. You can use the strategies presented i nWhat If...? Situation A to connect to how your students though about the percentages in the surbery questions. Then, use What If...? Situations B and C to assess how students can use percent thinking to make sense of a new situation. This problem will take 1.5 days to complete.
